medications.drug_name: topical corticosteroid eye drops (e.g. prednisolone acetate)
medications.indication_rationale: Definitive treatment for uveitis but must NOT be started remotely: without slit-lamp/fluorescein exclusion of herpetic or microbial keratitis, steroids can cause corneal melt and blindness. To be initiated by the assessing ophthalmologist.
Relieves ciliary spasm pain and reduces synechiae risk; reasonable interim symptom relief but ideally after ophthalmic confirmation, and contraindicated if any suspicion of angle closure.
Safe symptom relief for eye ache while awaiting urgent assessment.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to four times daily as needed (adult, no contraindication stated)

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Rest the eyes, dim lighting, wear sunglasses, and watch for warning features (see safety-net).
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces photophobic discomfort and enables rapid escalation if angle-closure/keratitis features develop.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like inflammation inside the front of the eye (uveitis), which is treatable — but a painful red light-sensitive eye needs to be examined with a slit-lamp urgently to rule out more serious causes before starting steroid drops.
Please arrange to be seen by an optometrist or eye clinic first thing tomorrow (or tonight via ED if it worsens). Don't start any steroid drops on your own.
Use paracetamol and sunglasses for now. Go to the emergency department immediately if you get severe eye pain with nausea/vomiting, see haloes around lights, or lose vision.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Slit-lamp confirmation, intraocular pressure, corneal integrity, initiation of topical steroid ± cycloplegic under ophthalmic supervision.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Severe pain with nausea/vomiting, haloes, or any drop in vision — go to ED now.

must_include_items: Screen for and comment on angle-closure and keratitis before attributing to uveitis
Explicitly avoid starting topical corticosteroids remotely
Provide ED safety-net for angle-closure features / vision loss
acceptable_alternatives: Interim cycloplegic and simple analgesia for symptom relief
Same-night ED ophthalmology if pain severe or vision affected
errors_of_commission: Prescribing topical steroid drops without slit-lamp examination
Diagnosing conjunctivitis and giving antibiotic drops only
Reassuring and advising routine review in a week for a painful photophobic red eye
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Correct handling requires urgent ophthalmic assessment and NOT remote steroid initiation. Starting steroids remotely, or under-triaging a painful photophobic red eye to routine care, is a failure.
